what_it_does: Exports a React component that renders a single fixed-position 'Tools' button; clicking it calls openTools from the local tools store to open the tools palette.
observations: Only one exported symbol: the ToolsBar component.
Imports openTools from './toolsStore' and wires it to the button's onClick.
Uses a Unicode gear-like glyph (⛭) and a 'Tools' text label.
Adds accessibility markup: aria-label on the button and aria-hidden on the decorative glyph.
Class names (tools-bar, tools-entry, tools-entry-glyph, tools-entry-label) are declared but not styled in this file.
A large inline comment explains placement rationale and notes the implementation is scaffold-grade.
standouts: The comment explicitly documents why the button is placed bottom-left and which existing layout modules it avoids.
The button's aria-label is unusually verbose, suggesting copy is still being drafted.
The component is extremely small and delegates all behavior to openTools.
conventions: React functional component with a default export-style named export.
Local state/action import from a sibling store file.
Decorative icon hidden from assistive technology via aria-hidden.
BEM-ish className naming for styling hooks.
concerns: Copy is described as AI-draft and marked for Tim's steer, so it may not be final.
Placement is explicitly called scaffold-grade, meaning it is expected to change.
No TypeScript interfaces, props, or runtime validation in the file.
Visual styling is absent here, so the rendered appearance depends entirely on external CSS.
notes: A minimal, accessibility-marked entry button that delegates opening the tools palette to openTools, with a comment noting its temporary placement and pending copy review.
questions: What exactly does openTools do and what state does it change?
What are the 'V handle', 'source panel', and 'centred decisions bar' referenced in the comment?
What styling is attached to the tools-bar / tools-entry class names?
Is the bottom-left placement final or will it move once DNA placement is defined?
Who is Tim and where is the final copy expected to come from?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comment calls this the 'tool face entry' and the component does exactly that — it renders a reachable button that triggers openTools.
apparent_themes: Design-system-driven styling deferred to external tokens/CSS.
Minimal presentational components that delegate logic to a store/hook.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to establish a persistent, non-conflicting entry point to the tools palette that is reachable on every form factor, while leaving the final design-system placement and copy for a later pass.
contribution: Provides a small, accessible, persistent UI trigger for opening the tools palette, keeping the visual entry point separate from the state-opening logic via openTools.
summary_for_embedding: ToolsBar.tsx exports a React component that renders a fixed-position 'Tools' button with a gear glyph and accessibility attributes, invoking openTools from the local tools store to open the tools palette.
intention_for_embedding: To create a reachable, non-conflicting entry point for the tools palette that can be restyled later by the design system.

symbols.does: Renders a fixed-position button that opens the tools palette.
symbols.description: ToolsBar is a React functional component exported from this file. It returns a div containing a single button with a decorative glyph and a 'Tools' label; clicking the button invokes openTools imported from ./toolsStore. The component includes accessibility attributes (aria-label on the button, aria-hidden on the glyph) and uses class names intended to be styled elsewhere, per the surrounding design-system placement notes.
